title: STAR Architecture Decision - Enigma Hybrid Retrieval
content:
Architecture Decision: Hybrid retrieval over single-mode search

Situation:
- Business discovery and document tasks needed both strict constraints and semantic understanding.
- Single-mode retrieval approaches produced either low recall or low precision depending on query type.

Task:
- Choose an architecture that preserves hard business constraints while still capturing semantic intent.

Action:
- Evaluated retrieval modes and selected a hybrid pattern: structured filtering plus vector retrieval and reranking.
- Separated query intent into hard filters and semantic components for better control.
- Implemented ranking refinement to improve final answer relevance.
- Added monitoring hooks to tune retrieval behavior with real usage feedback.

Result:
- Source narrative indicates stronger search usability and practical workflow performance.
- Decision became a reference architecture for retrieval-heavy product features.
